description_long: This is a freestanding water cooler with a rectangular, metallic gray base and a large, translucent blue water bottle mounted on top. The base features a recessed dispensing area with two distinct spouts, likely for hot and cold water, and a small drip tray beneath. The water bottle is ribbed and slightly frosted, giving it a textured appearance, and is securely inverted into the top of the cooler. The front panel of the cooler has two push-button or paddle-style dispensers, each aligned with a spout. The overall design is utilitarian and typical for office or public space hydration stations, with no visible branding or decorative elements. The surfaces appear smooth and hard, with a slight sheen on the plastic and metal components.
description: A metallic gray water cooler with a large blue water bottle on top and two dispensing spouts on the front. The design is functional, with a recessed area for filling cups and a drip tray below.
description_view_0: Side view showing the ribbed blue water bottle and the rectangular gray base, with the spouts and control panel not visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the two dispensing spouts, push-button or paddle controls, and the drip tray beneath the bottle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the bottle and base profile without the dispensing area.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the plain back of the cooler, with no visible controls or spouts.
attribute_control_panel_design: Two push-button or paddle controls aligned with the spouts, no visible indicators or displays.
